label: Registrars
description: an organization that allows registration of domains under a TLD - https://www.icann.org/en/icann-acronyms-and-terms/registrar-en

label: Registries
description: organizations responsible for maintaining the database of domains for a TLD - https://www.icann.org/en/icann-acronyms-and-terms/registry-en

label: Authoritative Operators
description: https://www.icann.org/en/icann-acronyms-and-terms/authoritative-name-server-en. In the context of this document, mitigation and prevention refers to the primary authoritative server.

label: Recursive Operators
description: Organizations operating either a private or public recursive resolver

label: Network Operators
description: Organizations operating an autonomous system (AS). We assume an organization with this capability is not running a recursive DNS server. This column means netflow and BGP data, and excludes (as a matter of a clarity choice here) passive DNS.

label: Application Service Provider
description: Software as a Service provider (like Google Docs), see https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#iso:std:iso-iec:17788:ed-1:v1:en for SaaS definition.

label: Hosting Provider
description: A company that provides Internet hosting services. "An Internet hosting service is a service that runs servers connected to the Internet, allowing organizations and individuals to serve content or host services connected to the Internet." See details: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_hosting_service

label: Threat Intelligence Provider
description: Threat intelligence providers aggregate, transform, analyze, interpret, or enrich intelligence to provide the necessary context for decision-making processes. CTI is considered as sharing and analysis only.

label: Device, OS, & Application Software Developers
description: Software developers who write the code or develop DNS resolver software or are responsible for updating an imported DNS resolver version in their software project.

label: Domain Registrants
description: “an individual or entity who registers a domain name” https://www.icann.org/en/icann-acronyms-and-terms/registrant-en. In the case of the malicious registration rows, this stakeholder is modeled as the actual human who made the malicious registration.

label: End User
description: Everyone who uses the Internet (who is not performing one of the other stakeholder capabilities listed).

label: Law Enforcement and Public Safety Authorities
description: Government organizations with authority to enforce laws or act in the public interest. Such organizations typically become aware of an issue because of:

label: CSIRTs
description: Computer Security Incident Response Teams. A Computer Security Incident Response Team is an organizational unit (which may be virtual) or a capability that provides services and support to a defined constituency for preventing, detecting, handling, and responding to computer security incidents, in accordance with its mission. (Taken from the Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) Services Framework v2.1) Each CSIRT can also be an end user of services, a registrant, threat intel provider, etc. When the CSIRT (organization) is performing those stakeholder capabilities, use respective columns. This column models exclusively the capability of the team.

label: Incident responder (internal)
description: The Computer Security Incident Response Team that is internal to the impacted organization.
